system:
Write a standalone Python script implementing the spec below.

Signature:  python tool.py <input_a.csv> <input_b.csv> <output.csv>
Robustness: the user may pass the two input files IN EITHER ORDER. The script must
            work out which file is which by reading each file's column headers,
            never by argv position.
Allowed:    pandas, and the Python standard library. Nothing else.
Forbidden:  network of any kind, subprocess, eval/exec, reading any file that isn't
            argv[1] or argv[2], writing any file that isn't argv[3], any AI/model call.
Style:      one `run()` function, constants at the top with the same names the user
            used, a comment above each block quoting the spec rule number it
            implements. This script is shown to the person who wrote the spec — the
            comments are for them.

You do NOT get to see the target file. Implement the spec, not the answer.

Return JSON only: {"code": "<script>", "headline": "<=14 words, plain English,
what you did and what you had to guess>"}

messages.content:
THE SPEC (frozen, approved by the user):
{
  "rules": [
    {
      "n": 1,
      "text": "Take two files from you: the day's manifest and the current rate card.",
      "confidence": 1.0,
      "source": "said"
    },
    {
      "n": 2,
      "text": "Throw away any run under 500 kg.",
      "confidence": 1.0,
      "source": "asked"
    },
    {
      "n": 3,
      "text": "Match each run to its truck's rate. If the truck isn't on the card, keep the run and write \"TBC\" where the cost goes.",
      "confidence": 1.0,
      "source": "asked"
    },
    {
      "n": 4,
      "text": "Work out the cost: the base fee, plus the rate per km times the distance. Round to whole dollars.",
      "confidence": 0.9,
      "source": "said"
    },
    {
      "n": 5,
      "text": "Turn kilos into tonnes, two decimal places.",
      "confidence": 0.85,
      "source": "said"
    },
    {
      "n": 6,
      "text": "Sort by destination A→Z, then dearest run first.",
      "confidence": 0.8,
      "source": "said"
    },
    {
      "n": 7,
      "text": "Name the columns your way: Date, Route, Truck, Load (t), Cost ($).",
      "confidence": 0.7,
      "source": "guessed"
    },
    {
      "n": 8,
      "text": "Put a TOTAL row at the bottom with the load and the cost added up, and nothing in the other columns.",
      "confidence": 0.9,
      "source": "said"
    }
  ],
  "guesses": [
    "Dates like 17.07.2026, because that's how your files are written.",
    "A comma in the thousands, no cents."
  ],
  "output_columns": [
    "Date",
    "Route",
    "Truck",
    "Load (t)",
    "Cost ($)"
  ]
}

THE INPUT FILES (first rows shown; these are the two files the script receives as argv[1] and argv[2], in this order):

# carrier_rates_2026-07.csv (6 rows)
carrier_code,carrier_name,truck_id,base_fee_usd,rate_per_km_usd,currency,valid_from
VLY,Valley Haul Co,TRK-03,240.00,1.85,USD,2026-07-01
PCF,Pacific Freightways,TRK-11,310.00,2.10,USD,2026-07-01
GRN,Grand Line Trucking,TRK-07,180.00,1.40,USD,2026-07-01
SRA,Sierra Cartage,TRK-22,265.00,1.95,USD,2026-07-01
DLT,Delta Road LLC,TRK-15,205.00,1.62,USD,2026-07-01

# manifest_2026-07-14.csv (13 rows)
shipment_id,date,origin,destination,cargo_type,weight_kg,distance_km,carrier_code
SHP-1041,2026-07-14,Oakland,Fresno,palletised,1240,300,PCF
SHP-1042,2026-07-14,Oakland,Bakersfield,palletised,480,460,VLY
SHP-1043,2026-07-14,Stockton,Fresno,bulk,2900,210,VLY
SHP-1044,2026-07-14,Oakland,Sacramento,refrigerated,860,130,GRN
SHP-1045,2026-07-14,Fremont,Bakersfield,palletised,3150,440,SRA

Write the script now. Return JSON only.

response:
{"code":"#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys
from decimal import Decimal, ROUND_HALF_UP

import pandas as pd

# Rule 7: "Name the columns your way: Date, Route, Truck, Load (t), Cost ($)."
DATE = "Date"
ROUTE = "Route"
TRUCK = "Truck"
LOAD_T = "Load (t)"
COST_USD = "Cost ($)"
OUTPUT_COLUMNS = [DATE, ROUTE, TRUCK, LOAD_T, COST_USD]

# Rule 1: "Take two files from you: the day's manifest and the current rate card."
MANIFEST_COLUMNS = {
    "date", "origin", "destination", "weight_kg", "distance_km", "carrier_code"
}
RATE_CARD_COLUMNS = {
    "carrier_code", "truck_id", "base_fee_usd", "rate_per_km_usd"
}


def run():
    # Rule 1: "Take two files from you: the day's manifest and the current rate card."
    if len(sys.argv) != 4:
        raise SystemExit("Usage: python tool.py <input_a.csv> <input_b.csv> <output.csv>")

    input_a, input_b, output_path = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2], sys.argv[3]
    headers_a = set(pd.read_csv(input_a, nrows=0).columns)
    headers_b = set(pd.read_csv(input_b, nrows=0).columns)

    a_is_manifest = MANIFEST_COLUMNS.issubset(headers_a)
    b_is_manifest = MANIFEST_COLUMNS.issubset(headers_b)
    a_is_rate_card = RATE_CARD_COLUMNS.issubset(headers_a)
    b_is_rate_card = RATE_CARD_COLUMNS.issubset(headers_b)

    if a_is_manifest and b_is_rate_card and not (a_is_rate_card or b_is_manifest):
        manifest_path, rate_card_path = input_a, input_b
    elif b_is_manifest and a_is_rate_card and not (b_is_rate_card or a_is_manifest):
        manifest_path, rate_card_path = input_b, input_a
    else:
        raise ValueError(
            "Could not uniquely identify one manifest and one rate card from their column headers."
        )

    manifest = pd.read_csv(manifest_path)
    rate_card = pd.read_csv(rate_card_path)

    # Rule 2: "Throw away any run under 500 kg."
    manifest["weight_kg"] = pd.to_numeric(manifest["weight_kg"], errors="raise")
    manifest["distance_km"] = pd.to_numeric(manifest["distance_km"], errors="raise")
    manifest = manifest.loc[manifest["weight_kg"] >= 500].copy()

    # Rule 3: "Match each run to its truck's rate. If the truck isn't on the card, keep the run and write \"TBC\" where the cost goes."
    if rate_card["carrier_code"].duplicated().any():
        raise ValueError("The rate card has more than one rate for at least one carrier_code.")
    rate_card["base_fee_usd"] = pd.to_numeric(rate_card["base_fee_usd"], errors="raise")
    rate_card["rate_per_km_usd"] = pd.to_numeric(rate_card["rate_per_km_usd"], errors="raise")
    runs = manifest.merge(
        rate_card[["carrier_code", "truck_id", "base_fee_usd", "rate_per_km_usd"]],
        on="carrier_code",
        how="left",
        validate="many_to_one",
    )

    # Rule 4: "Work out the cost: the base fee, plus the rate per km times the distance. Round to whole dollars."
    known_rate = runs["truck_id"].notna()
    runs["_cost_number"] = pd.NA
    runs.loc[known_rate, "_cost_number"] = runs.loc[known_rate].apply(
        lambda row: int(
            (
                Decimal(str(row["base_fee_usd"]))
                + Decimal(str(row["rate_per_km_usd"])) * Decimal(str(row["distance_km"]))
            ).quantize(Decimal("1"), rounding=ROUND_HALF_UP)
        ),
        axis=1,
    )

    # Rule 5: "Turn kilos into tonnes, two decimal places."
    runs["_load_number"] = runs["weight_kg"].apply(
        lambda kilograms: (Decimal(str(kilograms)) / Decimal("1000")).quantize(
            Decimal("0.01"), rounding=ROUND_HALF_UP
        )
    )

    # Rule 6: "Sort by destination A→Z, then dearest run first."
    runs["_cost_sort"] = pd.to_numeric(runs["_cost_number"], errors="coerce").fillna(float("-inf"))
    runs = runs.sort_values(
        ["destination", "_cost_sort"],
        ascending=[True, False],
        kind="mergesort",
        na_position="last",
    )

    # Rule 7: "Name the columns your way: Date, Route, Truck, Load (t), Cost ($)."
    report = pd.DataFrame(
        {
            DATE: pd.to_datetime(runs["date"], errors="raise").dt.strftime("%d.%m.%Y"),
            ROUTE: runs["origin"].astype(str) + " → " + runs["destination"].astype(str),
            TRUCK: runs["truck_id"].fillna(""),
            LOAD_T: runs["_load_number"].apply(lambda value: format(value, ",.2f")),
            COST_USD: runs["_cost_number"].apply(
                lambda value: "TBC" if pd.isna(value) else format(int(value), ",d")
            ),
        }
    )

    # Rule 8: "Put a TOTAL row at the bottom with the load and the cost added up, and nothing in the other columns."
    total_load = sum(runs["_load_number"], Decimal("0.00")).quantize(Decimal("0.01"))
    total_cost = int(pd.to_numeric(runs["_cost_number"], errors="coerce").fillna(0).sum())
    total_row = pd.DataFrame(
        [{DATE: "TOTAL", ROUTE: "", TRUCK: "", LOAD_T: format(total_load, ",.2f"), COST_USD: format(total_cost, ",d")}]
    )
    report = pd.concat([report[OUTPUT_COLUMNS], total_row[OUTPUT_COLUMNS]], ignore_index=True)

    # Rule 1: "Take two files from you: the day's manifest and the current rate card."
    report.to_csv(output_path, index=False)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    run()
","headline":"Built manifest-rate report; assumed carrier codes identify each truck's rate."}
